questions.question: What is the WLC management interface used for?
options: Management traffic (Telnet/SSH/HTTP/HTTPS, NTP, RADIUS, Syslog) and CAPWAP tunnel endpoint with APs
Only for web authentication splash pages
questions.explanation: The management interface is the virtual interface used for management traffic (Telnet/SSH/HTTP/HTTPS, NTP, RADIUS, Syslog); also the endpoint of CAPWAP tunnels with APs.

questions.question: What is a dynamic interface on a WLC?
options: WLC logical interface that maps a WLAN to a wired VLAN
Interface bound to the physical service port
Interface used for DHCP relay and web auth
Interface used to manage the standby WLC
questions.explanation: Dynamic interface is a WLC logical interface that maps a WLAN to a wired VLAN; each WLAN normally maps to one dynamic interface.

questions.question: Which WPA2 mode uses a pre-shared key for authentication?
questions.explanation: WPA2-Personal (PSK) uses a pre-shared key for authentication. WPA2-Enterprise uses a RADIUS server.

questions.question: What is the minimum length of an ASCII PSK?
questions.explanation: ASCII PSK must be at least 8 characters. PSK may be entered in ASCII or HEX.

questions.question: Which QoS profile is best for voice traffic on a WLC?
questions.explanation: WLC QoS levels: Platinum=voice, Gold=video, Silver=best effort (default), Bronze=background.

questions.question: Which QoS profile is the default for best-effort traffic?
questions.explanation: Silver is best-effort (default). Bronze is background, Gold video, Platinum voice.

questions.question: What is the difference between Profile Name and SSID on a WLC?
options: Profile name identifies the WLAN on the WLC; SSID is broadcast on air (typically identical but can differ)
SSID is for Layer 2; Profile Name is for Layer 3
Profile Name is only for 802.1X; SSID is only for PSK
They are always identical and cannot differ
questions.explanation: Profile name identifies the WLAN on the WLC; SSID is broadcast on air; typically identical but can differ.

questions.question: Which Layer 3 security option requires a user to enter credentials on a web portal?
questions.explanation: Web Authentication is Layer 3 security on WLC; requires user to enter credentials on a web portal. Web Passthrough shows a splash page with no credentials.

questions.question: What is the regulatory domain on a Cisco AP?
options: Letter code in the AP model (e.g., -A Americas, -E Europe); WLC country setting must match or AP won't join
A configuration mode for channel selection
The VTP domain assigned to the AP
questions.explanation: Regulatory domain is a letter code in the AP model (e.g., -A Americas, -E Europe); the WLC's country setting must match or the AP won't join.

questions.question: What does Auto-RF do?
options: WLC automatically picks channels and transmit power per AP
WLC automatically joins a cloud dashboard
questions.explanation: Auto-RF: the WLC automatically picks channels and transmit power per AP.

questions.question: What does 802.1X provide in a WPA2-Enterprise setup?
options: Port-based network access control using a RADIUS server for per-user authentication
VLAN tagging on the wireless side
questions.explanation: 802.1X is port-based network access control used in WPA-Enterprise. WPA2-Enterprise uses a RADIUS server for per-user authentication.

questions.question: Where are Clients listed on the WLC GUI?
questions.explanation: Clients page: Monitor → Clients lists associated clients, IP, AP, SSID.
